expectations.text: Targets Azure Resource Graph tables or consumption/billing tables
expectations.evidence: Part 1 uses 'resources' table in Azure Resource Graph querying 'microsoft.capacity/reservationorders/reservations'. Part 2 also uses 'resources' and references consumption via CLI commands. The header explicitly states 'Run this in Azure Resource Graph Explorer (portal) or via az graph query'.

expectations.text: Projects reservation name/display name in results
expectations.evidence: 'ReservationName = tostring(name)' is projected in both Part 1 and Part 2 of the query

expectations.text: Shows utilization percentage for each reservation
expectations.evidence: 'UtilizationPct = round(todouble(UtilizationEntry.value) * 100, 2)' derived from properties.utilizationAggregates with P7D grain filter

expectations.text: Shows which resources are consuming each reservation
expectations.evidence: Part 2 joins reservations with 'consumingResources' via typeMap, projecting 'ConsumingResource = ResourceName' and 'ConsumingResourceId = ResourceId'

expectations.text: Includes Azure CLI commands (az graph query or az consumption) to run the query
expectations.evidence: run-via-azure-cli.sh includes 10 sections with 'az graph query' commands, 'az consumption reservation summary list', 'az consumption reservation detail list', and 'az reservations reservation-order list'

expectations.text: Output includes .kql file with comment header
expectations.evidence: reservation-consumption-analysis.kql opens with structured header block containing Title, Service, Tables, Description, Parameters (reservationScope, utilizationThreshold, lookbackDays), Complexity: Advanced

claims.claim: Includes IsUnderutilized flag and configurable utilizationThreshold
claims.evidence: 'let utilizationThreshold = 80.0;' and 'IsUnderutilized = iff(UtilizationPct < utilizationThreshold, true, false)'

claims.claim: Includes typeMap datatable to map reservation types to ARM resource types
claims.evidence: datatable(ReservedResourceType:string, ArmType:string) with 18 resource type mappings (VMs, SQL, PostgreSQL, CosmosDB, AppService, etc.)

claims.claim: CLI script includes pagination handling with --skip-token
claims.evidence: Section 3 of run-via-azure-cli.sh handles ARG's 1000-row limit with skip token iteration

eval_feedback.overall: All 6 assertions pass. The output is thorough with a comprehensive typeMap, CLI pagination, and scope-based resource filtering. No suggestions.
